content.full_text:
FPPC

FEB 14 9 34 AM '92

Councilman John M. Lane
1249 Riverside Drive
Rio Dell, CA 95562
tel (707) 764-5004
February 10, 1992

California Fair Political Practices Commission
Attn: Ms Alice Hughes
       Political Reform Consultant
428 J Street  Suite 800
PO Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Ms Hughes:

Thank you for your guidance (file I-91-472) on our filing obligations as
elected officeholders subject to a recall election on April 14, 1992.
I am happy to report that our filing obligations have been met thanks to
your very clear guidance.

In reading Campaign Information Manual "D" (August 1991) page 52,
Information for Ballot Measure Committees the question has arisen whether
the Proponents of our recall (all non-office holders) have an obligation to
also report to the Commission.

The declared Proponents are:

Chairperson: Valerie S. Creel  246 Tolman Place   Rio Dell, CA 95562
Glenna M. Nielsen  1 Painter Street  Space 32   Rio Dell, CA 95562
Terry M. Alfred  751 Pacific Avenue   Rio Dell, CA 95562
Connie Griffith   386- #B Center Street    Rio Dell, CA 95562
Adelbert C. Miller   170 - 2nd Avenue    Rio Dell, CA 95562

Significant funds have been spent by the Proponents in newspaper
advertisements and distribution of pro recall data.

Would you please advise me whether the Proponents also have to report
their campaign activity. If they do, would they file with our City Clerk?
Do Commission records reflect that the Proponents' Committee have filed?

Please telephone me at (707) 764-5004 if any additional data is
required.


Sincerely,

John M. Lane
Councilman, City of Rio Dell

[The image appears to be a heavily faded or low-quality photocopy/scan of a typed document. Most of the text is illegible or extremely difficult to read. I can make out only scattered fragments:]

[illegible] is [illegible] specifications
[illegible] shipped [illegible]
[illegible] to [illegible] oil?
[illegible] by [illegible] feet
[illegible]

[Multiple sections of heavily faded text that are not clearly legible]

[The document continues with numerous lines that are too faint or unclear to transcribe accurately]

[Near the bottom, there appears to be a signature or marking that looks like "Virginity" or similar]

[The overall document quality makes reliable full transcription impossible without being able to read the specific text]
